members.title:
Executive Committee Member
Dr. Apeksha Ekbote
members.details:
Dr. Apeksha Ekbote is Chief Dietitian at NephroPlus, leading nutritional care for dialysis patients across India for the past five years.
With over 17 years of experience in clinical nutrition and academia, she brings a wealth of knowledge and expertise to the field. She is a Ph.D. in Nutrition from Osmania University, Registered Dietitian, and a certified Diabetes Educator. A double gold medalist in her master’s program in Nutrition, Dr. Apeksha Ekbote’s professional interests lie in Renal Nutrition, Critical Care, Metabolic Disorders, and Weight Management. Has presented papers in National and International conferences which has made her awards. She is a Sadhana Awardee 2024 in young dietician category.
In addition to her clinical work, she is actively engaged in research focused on dialysis patients and has published seven papers in both national and international journals on different topics. A true polyglot, she is fluent in eight languages. Beyond her professional pursuits, she enjoys art and craft, baking, and continuously enhancing her knowledge—most recently exploring the intersection of nutrition and artificial intelligence.

members.details: Dr. Ratna Mulay, a distinguished paediatrician and public health expert with 30+ years of experience, holds degrees in Medicine, Adolescent Health, Health Management, Nutrition, and a Ph.D. in Women’s Studies. She has led major health programs with the Govt. of Madhya Pradesh, including IDSP, RNTCP, and India’s first measles surveillance. She also contributed significantly during COVID-19 with the Dept. of Health Research, Govt. of India. An advisor to government bodies and NGOs, she has published widely on health and gender, authored “Women – Malnourished Minds & Broken Bodies”, and co-edited a book on paediatric malnutrition. Beyond work, she enjoys reading, travelling, and painting.

Dr. Mumtaz Khalid Ismail is a Consultant Clinical Dietician with over 33 years of experience in dietetics, clinical nutrition, research, and public health. A graduate of Aligarh Muslim University with a PhD in Foods and Nutrition from Mother Theresa Women’s University, she has specialized training in sports nutrition, diabetes education, and FODMAP diet therapy for IBS.
She has served in reputed institutions including the Army Medical Corps, Apollo Hospitals, UNICEF, and the National Rural Health Mission, and was instrumental in drafting Kerala’s Nutrition Policy in 2013.
Dr. Mumtaz has also set up Kerala’s first government hospital dietary department at Ernakulam General Hospital.Currently, she practices at Rainbow Polyclinic, Total Nutrition Plus as online and offline consultation and is a professor of nutrition in sree sudheendra college of nursing and Lisie college of Nursing, and consults for corporates on wellness and sustainable nutrition practices.
An accomplished researcher, columnist, and speaker, she is actively involved with professional associations including the Indian Dietetic Association, Nutrition Society of India, Association for Evidence Based Dietetics and Nutrition, Womens Wing of Indian Medical Association and serves as Secretary of Women Entrepreneurs Network Foundation.
Her key areas of interest include women’s nutrition, lifestyle disorders, infant and child nutrition, sports nutrition, corporate wellness, and public health.
